channels.purpose: Generates PKCE challenge, opens browser for GitHub authorization
Exchanges OAuth code for token via PKCE, stores encrypted
Decrypts and returns stored access token
Encrypts and stores a manually-provided token
Restricted HTTP proxy for github.com API requests with auth

urls.purpose: OAuth authorization endpoint (base, built with query params)
Custom protocol redirect URI (hardcoded in 2 separate locations)

exports.name: registerGithubIpcHandlers
exports.parameters: none (no DI — unlike registerIpcHandlers which takes Deps)

store_namespace.name: github-auth
store_namespace.protection: listed in RESERVED_STORE_NAMES (ipc.ts:27) — blocked from generic store-get/set/delete handlers

blockers: File does not exist — broken import at ipc.ts:19
yellow_flags: Hardcoded client ID — no env var or channel override, fork-hostile
Hardcoded redirect_uri duplicated in two locations
No channel differentiation (dev/beta/prod share same GitHub App)
In-memory pendingOAuth Map — lost on restart, stale browser tabs
O(n) state lookup iterates entire Map
Zero-dependency handler function — no DI, directly calls getStore/safeStorage, harder to test
No safeStorage fallback — throws if system keychain unavailable
API proxy errors serialized as JSON.stringify inside Error.message — type mismatch with preload bridge signature
green_flags: Good architectural extraction reducing ipc.ts from 503 to ~385 lines
Secure OAuth: PKCE flow (no client secret), SHA-256 code challenge
Encrypted token storage via Electron safeStorage
Store namespace protected from generic access

overall_pattern.architecture: Self-contained subsystem with module-level state + electron-store persistence + OS-level encryption
overall_pattern.auth_flow: PKCE OAuth 2.0 → encrypted token storage → authenticated API proxy
overall_pattern.persistence: electron-store named 'github-auth' with Electron safeStorage encryption
overall_pattern.security_model: Write: safeStorage.encryptString → base64 → store. Read: store → base64 → safeStorage.decryptString
overall_pattern.error_handling: Token retrieval: silent null on decrypt failure (auto-clears). API proxy: throws strings, not Error objects
